message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): oneshot mid-flight cancel via select-race pattern (tick 433)

Probes whether the tick-432 mpsc mid-flight-cancel-waker gap applies
to oneshot_recv_with_cx as well. Same test shape: spawn cancel task,
wrap recv in `futures::future::select` against a poll-sleep watcher.

**Outcome**: watcher branch fires consistently — confirms the same
gap applies to oneshot_recv_with_cx. asupersync's oneshot receiver
short-circuits on pre-cancel via per-poll cx.checkpoint() (tick 419
pins that), but does NOT register a cx-cancel-waker for an already-
suspended recv. Callers needing mid-flight cancel on oneshot must
use the same select-race pattern as tick 432 pinned for mpsc.

The match-arm structure tolerates a future improvement in asupersync
where oneshot recv itself gains cx-cancel-waker support — both
Either::Left(Err) and Either::Right(Err) branches are accepted as
valid outcomes, so the test stays green if observability improves.

Cross-primitive pattern now established:
- tick 422 + 419 pin pre-cancel on mpsc/oneshot
- tick 432 + 433 pin select-race mid-flight cancel pattern on
  mpsc/oneshot, documenting the shared waker-registration gap

Test name `_mid_flight_cancel_via_select_race_pattern` mirrors the
tick-432 naming convention so the two related tests group lexically.

Observed locally: 0.12 s wall — cancel at 100 ms, watcher observes
within 50 ms poll cycle.

Check script Run 6/6 filter `oneshot_recv_with_cx` already catches
the new test alongside the pre-cancel test — no script change needed.
Run 6 grows 17 → 18 tests. Total: 104 → 105.

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
